summary: Strong test suite with excellent workflow integration testing via act and comprehensive unit test coverage. Pester tests (18 total) cover all major functions: XML/JSON parsing, aggregation, flaky test detection, and markdown generation. Tests exercise the happy path, error cases (file not found, invalid XML), and conditional logic (flaky section presence/absence). However, missing edge case coverage: zero-count scenarios (executed=0 for pass rate, all-skipped results, no-failures), malformed test data, and boundary conditions. Design is well-organized with clear test names, efficient fixture reuse via BeforeAll, and excellent separation of unit tests (Aggregate-TestResults.Tests.ps1) from integration tests (run-tests.ps1). run-tests.ps1 validates workflow structure and runs full end-to-end tests through act with specific output assertions, which is excellent practice but relies on regex string matching (somewhat brittle). Critical dependency: tests assume fixture files (run1-junit.xml, run2-junit.xml, run3-results.json) exist but fixture setup is not shown—tests will fail if fixtures are missing or malformed. Overall: test suite would reliably catch most regressions in the main functionality and workflow integration, but might miss bugs related to edge cases and boundary conditions.
